reads + writes

reads1 writes14 fsyncs1
Flushing breakdown ▸

peak snapshot 2026_04_21_16_52_11

Pathpeakwhat it means
LRU flush 7 Buffer-pool free list exhausted — eviction path backlog.
Flush list 7 Checkpoint-age pressure — redo is close to its limit.
Single page – Synchronous stall — a user thread waited on a clean page.
fsync · log – Redo fsync backlog — commits are waiting on disk.
fsync · buffer pool 1 Page cleaners can't keep up with the I/O layer.
Modified pages 8,524 Dirty-page backlog across buffer pool instances.

LRU / flush-list / single-page split shows which flushing path is backed up. Single-page flushes > 0 and pending log fsyncs > 0 are the user-visible stall signals.

AHI

0.0%

hit ratio

min 0.0% · avg 0.0% · max 0.0%

Formula ▸

hit ratio = hash / (hash + non-hash)

at worst = 0.00 / (0.00 + 3689000.00) = 0.0%

worst snapshot 2026_04_21_16_52_11 · hash table size 138,401

A high hit ratio means most index lookups are served directly from the adaptive hash index; a low one means InnoDB is falling back to B-tree traversal — consider whether AHI's memory is being paid for.
